findings.context: information is becoming available online in the form of video. However, for people with disabilities ( **= 15% of the world's population**), accessing and consuming video content can be a challenge. For us as

location.heading: 12 Ways to Make Accessible Video Content

findings.sentence: However, for people with disabilities ( **= 15% of the world's population**), accessing and consuming video content can be a challenge.

meta.anchor: W3C's guide on creating accessible audio and video

findings.sentence: For more information, please refer to [W3C's guide on creating accessible audio and video](https://www.w3.org/WAI/media/av/).
The color contrast ratio for [WCAG level AAA](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/contrast-enhanced) compliance is **4.5:1**.
However, the minimum acceptable color contrast ratio is **3:1**, if you're aiming for [WCAG level AA compliance](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/contrast-minimum.html).
If you, like most other people, can't measure color contrast ratio by eye, use this handy [Color Contrast Checker](https://coolors.co/contrast-checker/112a46-acc8e5).
For a more detailed explanation, read [this guide](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/low-or-no-background-audio.html) on the 'Low or No Background Audio' criterion.
Captions, as defined by [W3C](https://www.w3.org/WAI/media/av/captions/), "are text versions of the speech and non-speech audio information needed to understand the content."
The [W3C](https://www.w3.org/WAI/media/av/description/) distinguishes between 3 types of audio descriptions:

However, if you need to describe important visual details that are essential to understand the video, an audio description is required for [WCAG level AA compliance](https://www.w3.org/WAI/media/av/description/#checklist).

meta.anchor: W3C's guidelines for creating audio descriptions

findings.sentence: Instructions for creating audio descriptions differ depending on the type you choose to go with, so check [W3C's guidelines for creating audio descriptions](https://www.w3.org/WAI/media/av/description/#options-for-creating-description).
Descriptive transcripts are required for video with audio content to be [WCAG Level AAA compliant](https://www.w3.org/WAI/media/av/transcripts/#checklist).
You can find more information on how to transcribe videos on [W3C's website](https://www.w3.org/WAI/media/av/transcribing/#how-to-transcribe).

Use automatic transcription and speech recognition software, like [Sonix](https://sonix.ai/), [Trint](https://trint.com/), or [Google Cloud Speech-To-Text](https://cloud.google.com/speech-to-text/) to make transcribing and captioning videos faster.

Use automatic transcription and speech recognition software, like [Sonix](https://sonix.ai/), [Trint](https://trint.com/), or [Google Cloud Speech-To-Text](https://cloud.google.com/speech-to-text/) to make transcribing and captioning videos faster.

Use automatic transcription and speech recognition software, like [Sonix](https://sonix.ai/), [Trint](https://trint.com/), or [Google Cloud Speech-To-Text](https://cloud.google.com/speech-to-text/) to make transcribing and captioning videos faster.

To better understand the intent and benefits of this recommendation, check out WCAG's detailed guide on the ' [Three Flashes and Below Threshold](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/three-flashes-or-below-threshold.html)'.
